heading: Office of Marine Forces Reserve: appointment of Commander
label: 8084 Office of Marine Forces Reserve: appointment of Commander

children.heading: .—Establishment of Office; Commander, Marine Forces Reserve
children.label: a .—Establishment of Office; Commander, Marine Forces Reserve
children.text: There is in the executive part of the Department of the Navy an Office of the Marine Forces Reserve, which is headed by the Commander, Marine Forces Reserve. The Commander, Marine Forces Reserve, is the principal adviser to the Commandant on Marine Forces Reserve matters.

The President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, shall appoint the Commander, Marine Forces Reserve, from general officers of the Marine Corps Reserve who have had at least 10 years of commissioned service.

children.label: A
children.text: is recommended by the Secretary of the Navy; and

children.label: B
children.text: is determined by the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, in accordance with criteria and as a result of a process established by the Chairman, to have significant joint duty experience.
An officer on active duty for service as the Commander, Marine Forces Reserve, shall be counted for purposes of the grade limitations under sections 525 and 526 of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: the officer is qualified for service in the position; and

children.label: B
children.text: the waiver is necessary for the good of the service.

children.heading: .—Term; Reappointment
children.label: c .—Term; Reappointment
children.text: The Commander, Marine Forces Reserve, is appointed for a term determined by the Commandant of the Marine Corps, normally four years, but may be removed for cause at any time. An officer serving as Commander, Marine Forces Reserve, may be reappointed for one additional term of up to four years.

children.heading: .—Annual Report
children.label: d .—Annual Report
children.text: The Commander, Marine Forces Reserve, shall submit to the Secretary of Defense, through the Secretary of the Navy, an annual report on the state of the Marine Corps Reserve and the ability of the Marine Corps Reserve to meet its missions. The report shall be prepared in conjunction with the Commandant of the Marine Corps and may be submitted in classified and unclassified versions.
